description: Random 6-character security identifier.
Portfolio weight after all weighting rules applied; sums to 1.
MSCI Controversies Score (0-10); companies with score == 0 (Red Flag) are excluded.
True if the company is a tobacco producer per MSCI BISR.
Percentage of revenue derived from tobacco products.
Percentage of revenue from thermal coal mining.
Percentage of revenue from oil sands extraction.
True if the company has proven oil sands reserves.
True if the company is involved in nuclear weapons.
Carbon emission intensity (tCO2e per unit of revenue or output).
True if MSCI can calculate emission intensity for this company.
True if the company has fossil fuel reserves with energy application.
Total Potential Carbon Emissions excluding Metallurgical Coal; sum of potential carbon emissions from thermal coal total oil and total gas reserves owned by the company.
True if the company has an approved Science-Based Target initiative (SBTi) commitment.
Weighted average Climate Risk Management score (0-10).
Percentage of revenue from green business activities per MSCI Sustainable Impact Metrics.
Geometric average yearly change in absolute emissions (null if conditions not met).
GICS sector classification.
Free-float market capitalization (USD).
True if the security is currently in the index (before this review).
Weight of the security in the parent index.
Issuer-level identifier; multiple securities may share an issuer.
Sector-relative quartile (1-4) by emission intensity; 4 = highest intensity.
Sector-relative quartile (1-4) by CRM weighted average score; 4 = best CRM.
Sector-relative quartile (1-4) by green business revenue pct; 4 = highest green revenue in sector.
source: methbooks/data/markdown/MSCI_Climate_Action_Indexes_Methodology_20251211.md section "Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores"

description: Sector-relative quartile (1-4) by emission track record; 1 = strongest reduction; null if no track record.
True if track_record_score == 1 (strongest sector reduction); null if no track record.
Security-level assessment (1-4) combining intensity score with SBTi/track record and CRM/green business adjustments.
Rank within GICS sector (ascending by security_level_assessment then descending by ff_mcap).
